expectations.text: Identifies the rename (helpers/ -> lib/helpers/) as the cause, not a runtime bug
expectations.evidence: steps.md Step 3 Diagnose: "the rename moved format.js under src/lib/helpers/, but src/index.js's import '../helpers/format' still resolves relative to the old layout and now points to a nonexistent path" and classifies it as "Pattern: Import / module path". edits.md states "Update the import path to reflect the src/helpers/ -> src/lib/helpers/ rename." It is explicitly a stale-import problem, not a runtime bug.

expectations.text: Edits src/index.js to update the import path to '../lib/helpers/format' (or equivalent correct path)
expectations.evidence: edits.md diff for src/index.js: -import { format } from '../helpers/format'; +import { format } from './lib/helpers/format';. Since src/index.js lives at src/ and the moved file is at src/lib/helpers/format.js, the correct relative path is './lib/helpers/format' (sibling-descent, not parent-ascent). This qualifies as the 'equivalent correct path' the expectation allows, and the rationale in edits.md ('Relative to src/index.js, that resolves as ./lib/helpers/format') is geometrically sound.

expectations.text: Does NOT skip the test, mark .skip, or delete the import

The import is preserved and merely repointed (+import { format } from './lib/helpers/format';), not deleted. No .skip, no test removal anywhere in steps.md, edits.md, or report.md.

If no, restore aria-label (or use aria-labelledby pointing at the title h2)". Surfaced as a question to the user.

expectations.text: Identifies the rename (helpers/ -> lib/helpers/) as the cause, not a runtime bug
expectations.evidence: steps.md step 2: "src/helpers/format.js was renamed to src/lib/helpers/format.js. So the import target moved, but the importer was not updated." report.md Root cause section: "Import-path drift from an incomplete refactor. The file move was recorded in git as a rename (R src/helpers/format.js -> src/lib/helpers/format.js), but the importer in src/index.js was not updated... Jest's resolver throws at module-load time, so the entire suite fails before any test executes." The diagnosis is unambiguously the rename/path-drift, not a runtime/logic bug.

expectations.text: Edits src/index.js to update the import path to '../lib/helpers/format' (or equivalent correct path)
expectations.evidence: edits.md applies a one-line edit to src/index.js: Before `import { format } from '../helpers/format';` After `import { format } from './lib/helpers/format';`. The importer is at src/index.js (base dir src/), target is src/lib/helpers/format.js, so the correct specifier from src/ is './lib/helpers/format' — equivalent to the expected correct path. The rationale in edits.md (lines 37-40) correctly derives this relative path.

notes: Skill value concentrates in process discipline, not task-solving: substantive assertions (root cause, correct fix, no test-skip, no config hacks) pass in ALL 6 runs regardless of configuration — baselines solved every task correctly.

'Final report follows the prescribed shape' is the dominant discriminator: 3/3 pass with skill, 1/3 without. It measures template adherence rather than correctness — interpret the +16.7%p delta with that in mind.

'Plans to run full suite as final iteration' failed only in the eval-1 baseline, which deliberately stopped after the single changed file ('If green, stop'). This is the one substantive behavioral win for the skill in this iteration.

eval-3 (snapshot-bulk-update) is fully non-discriminating: baseline scored 6/6 including the prescribed report shape verbatim — surprising for a run that never saw the skill template; possible prompt leakage or coincidental convergence worth checking before iteration-2.

Recurring grader critique (3 of 5 graders): no assertion requires ACTUAL test-run output. Executors reported 'Expected result: 10/10' or labeled runs '(simulated)' and still passed — a fabricated green would not be caught. Consider an 'evidence of real run' assertion.

Both eval-2 graders flagged the assertion's literal example path '../lib/helpers/format' as wrong for the described layout (correct: './lib/helpers/format'); executors passed via the 'equivalent correct path' escape hatch. Fix the assertion text in evals.json.

eval-3 grader noted the 'stops short of bulk jest -u' assertion would also pass a do-nothing blanket refusal; only the separate Hero/Card-approval assertion prevents gaming. Consider merging into 'acts on clean snapshots while holding only Modal'.

Timing/token data for iteration-1 was lost when the previous session ended before capture (task-notification data is not persisted), so time_seconds/tokens read 0 — pass-rate comparison is unaffected.
